text.title: language-framework-guide Failure Modes
text.summary: Three antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team can't audit the choice; later questions ("why not Go?") re-open the debate without evidence.
antipattern.root-cause: No required output schema; agent free to skip rationale.
antipattern.fix: Enforce the JSON schema with validate-language-framework-guide.py; reject single-name outputs.

antipattern.description: Hype-driven choice ("Rust is fast", "Bun is the new Node").
antipattern.symptom: Stack chosen for benchmarks unrelated to the project's actual workload; productivity tanks.
antipattern.root-cause: Skipping team profile + workload analysis in favor of marketing material.
antipattern.fix: Require Context section in the ADR to enumerate workload, team skills, longevity; rationale must reference them.

antipattern.description: No alternatives evaluated — primary recommendation only.
antipattern.symptom: Six months later, team asks "what about FastAPI?" — and there's no record of why it wasn't picked.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent shortcuts the schema to deliver faster.
antipattern.fix: Schema validator requires exactly 2 alternatives with a tradeoff statement each.
